source: .specs/agent-scenarios.md → Feature: Synchronous slash command (no-LLM local command)
notes: In ONE live pi REPL session: a real prompt drives a single ready→working→ready arc on the rollout session, then a purely-local slash command (/session — 'Show session info and stats', verified live against pi 0.70.0) is issued. /session renders session stats (id, message counts, tokens, cost) directly in the TUI WITHOUT an LLM round-trip and writes NOTHING to the rollout transcript (verified live: transcript line count unchanged before/after /session). The whole observable is the ABSENCE of a second working transition — the session stays ready across the entire slash-command window. WHY interactive (not headless `pi --print`): a slash command only exists inside the REPL; headless pi is single-shot. WHY a warm-up turn FIRST: pi creates its rollout-*.jsonl only after the first user message, so resolve_transcript needs a real turn to bind the session; the warm-up also gives us the one legitimate working→ready arc to contrast against the slash command's silence. WHY the trailing sleep after the slash: it holds the recording open well past the classifier debounce so the post-slash ready dwell is observed cleanly and the daemon-kill doesn't race the final-state emission. C7 / SESSION-IDENTITY: the live REPL emits a transient proc-<PID> pre-session born ready at launch (bridges the gap until the UUID rollout transcript appears), so session_birth — which only asserts 'a session appears in ready within 2s of launch' — legitimately matches that pre-session (same shape as pi/session-start and pi/multi-turn-conversation). The turn arc and the post-slash dwell must land on the UUID rollout session: turn_start anchors to recording start with NO session pin so the matcher picks the FIRST working in the whole recording, which is necessarily the rollout session (the pre-session never goes working); turn_done, pid_bind, the slash dwell, and teardown then pin to turn_start's session via same_session_as. KEY ASSERTION: exactly ONE working→ready arc (the warm-up turn); the slash command adds NO additional working transition (state stays ready) and NO waiting; a single process_exited on the rollout session. HOW runs differ: warm-up assistant wording, per-turn token counts, session UUID, PID, and sub-second timing vary (model-output variance). Stable structurally: one ready→working→ready arc on one rollout session_id, a continuous ready dwell across the slash window with no further working transition, no waiting, a single process_exited, and a final ready state.

text: A session appears in ready within 2s of REPL launch
Warm-up prompt → working (binds the rollout session; the pre-session never enters working)
Warm-up reply completes → back to ready; state never entered waiting
PID bound to the rollout session
Local /session command responds in the TUI with no model call — the session stays ready throughout; no → working transition is recorded for the slash command, and state never entered waiting
REPL torn down once after the slash window → a single process_exited on the rollout session; state immediately before teardown is ready
